conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
So, you're from Memphis. How has your week been so far?
Oh, it's been kind of tough, honestly. I've been trying to stay focused at work but my attention keeps wandering.
Can you tell me more about what's been distracting you at work?
Yeah, sure thing. It's just that I'm finding myself getting really tired a lot lately. Even when I do get home, I don't have the energy to do much of anything.
That sounds frustrating. Have you noticed any changes in your daily routine or habits recently?
Honestly? I feel like I've been losing interest in things that usually bring me joy. Like playing guitar – it used to be my favorite way to unwind, but now it feels like a chore.
It's great that you're willing to explore this further. Can you tell me more about what's going on at work that's contributing to your fatigue?
Well, we've been short-staffed and I'm trying to pick up the slack, but it's exhausting me. And then there are these long hours of paperwork that I just can't seem to get through.
That sounds really tough. Have you talked to anyone at work about how you're feeling?
Yeah, I tried to have a conversation with my supervisor, but it didn't go so well. He just told me to "toughen up" and focus on the job.
I'm sorry to hear that. It sounds like your energy levels are really suffering as a result of this stress. How do you usually take care of yourself when you're feeling drained?
To be honest, not very well – I mean, I used to run regularly, but now it feels like too much effort.
That makes sense. It sounds like your guilt around not being able to keep up with your usual activities is really weighing on you.
Yeah, guilty feelings are definitely a big part of my emotional state right now.
Can you tell me more about what's going through your mind when you're feeling guilty?
I guess I feel like I'm letting everyone down – my colleagues, my family... it feels like nobody wants to hear that things are tough for me right now.
It sounds like you're carrying a lot of responsibility on your shoulders. What are some small steps you could take to start taking care of yourself and prioritizing your own needs?
Honestly? I'm not really sure – I feel like that would be selfish, but maybe just getting out the door for a walk without feeling like I have to check in with anyone else.
That sounds like a great place to start. And what are some things you'd like to achieve or accomplish in your life right now?
Honestly? I think I'd love to get back into playing music and maybe even take up painting again – it's just been really hard to muster the energy.
Those sound like great goals! What do you think would be the first step towards getting back to those activities?
Maybe just setting aside a specific time each day or week, like when I get home from work?
That sounds like a great plan. How does that make you feel, thinking about taking that kind of control back in your life?
Honestly? It gives me a little glimmer of hope.
That's wonderful! What are some things that give you a sense of accomplishment or pride in your daily life right now?
Actually, I do love working on cases and solving puzzles as a police officer. That always gives me a boost.
That's great! How can we build on that sense of pride and accomplishment to help you tackle the challenges you're facing?
Maybe just focusing on one case at a time, taking it one step at a time, and not beating myself up over mistakes – like I usually do.
That sounds like a great approach. And finally, what are your goals for this session today? What do you hope to take away from our conversation?
Honestly? I guess just having someone to talk to who doesn't judge me – and maybe getting some ideas on how to manage my energy levels better.
Well, we've covered a lot of ground today. Before we wrap up, is there anything specific you'd like to accomplish or change about your situation?
Yeah, I think just being able to acknowledge that it's okay to not be perfect – and maybe finding some ways to prioritize my own needs without feeling guilty.
That's a great goal! I'll summarize what we've discussed today, and we can build on these ideas in our next session.
